name: The Creamiest Tofu Cheesecake in the World
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup unrefined sugar crystals or sugar
recipeInstructions:
The butter or margarine is to be melted. Spectrum Spread may be substituted.
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. For the crust, in a bowl, combine the graham cracker crumbs with the ground almonds and sweetener. With a fork, blend the butter (or margarine) into the mixture until it clings together when you press a teaspoon of it between your fingers. Turn the mixture into an 8-inch springform pan. Using your fingers or the back of a soup spoon, spread and pat the crust until it covers the bottom of the pan. Keep working the crust up the sides of the pan to a height of about 1 inch, leaving it thicker around the bottom edge where the sides and bottom of the pan meet. Make sure the crust is thinner around the top edge. Bake the crust for 8 minutes. Set aside to cool slightly.
3. For the filling, puree the tofu in a food processor. Add the cream cheese and process to blend. Add the sugar and eggs and process to incorporate them completely. Blend in the lemon juice and vanilla. Finally, blend in the arrowroot powder.
4. Pour the filling into the prepared crust, filling it to within 1/2-inch of the top. Bake in the center of the oven for 60 minutes. The cake will be golden on top, slightly browned around the edges, and still puffed and jiggling in the center when removed from the oven. Turn off the heat but leave the cake sitting in the oven (it is okay to peek once or twice). The warm cake will still be soft to the touch in the center. Set the cake on a rack and let cool to room temperature. Cover lightly with foil and refrigerate for 24 hours before serving.
Notes: The cake will taste delicious once it is chilled through, but only reaches full creaminess after being refrigerated for 8 hours or overnight.
Spectrum Spread is a non-hydrogenated canola oil product made by Spectrum Naturals, Inc.
